args.query: site:ebay.com/help/policies/money-back-guarantee eBay item not as described no returns

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: eBay Money Back Guarantee policy

  - url: https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy?id=4210

  - source: eBay

  - domain: ebay.com


  ## Content


  # eBay Money Back Guarantee policy


  ## Item does not match the listing


  Sellers are required to deliver the item represented in the listing. When a buyer
  receives the wrong item, or the item arrives broken, damaged, faulty, or materially
  different from the listing description, the buyer may return it for a refund under
  eBay Money Back Guarantee if the transaction and request meet the policy requirements.


  This item-problem coverage is different from an ordinary change-of-mind return.
  A seller''s decision not to offer ordinary returns does not by itself remove coverage
  for a qualifying wrong-item or not-as-described problem.


  The buyer and seller may agree on another resolution, such as a replacement or a
  full or partial refund without a return. When an item is returned for a covered
  not-as-described reason, the seller is generally responsible for return shipping.


  ## Opening the request


  The buyer starts the return from the matching eBay order and selects the reason
  that describes the problem. For a not-as-described return, the request normally
  must be opened no later than 30 calendar days after the estimated or actual delivery
  date, or within a longer seller return window when one applies. Some categories
  have special or shorter coverage rules.


  The seller is expected to respond and provide a solution within the period shown
  in the request. If the item must be shipped back, the buyer must follow the return
  instructions and shipping deadline supplied through eBay.


  ## eBay review


  If the seller does not respond, does not provide a required solution, or does not
  issue the refund by the applicable deadline, the buyer or seller may ask eBay to
  step in.


  When reviewing a not-as-described case, eBay may consider whether each party met
  the return requirements, whether the listing complied with eBay policy, whether
  the item can be returned, and whether return delivery can be proven. The final outcome
  depends on transaction eligibility and the available evidence.


  Buyers should accurately describe the issue and preserve listing information, item
  photographs, model or condition details, and return tracking when relevant.

  '
metadata: {}
title: eBay Money Back Guarantee policy
url: https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy?id=4210

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Returns and refunds | eBay

  - url: https://www.ebay.com/help/returns-refunds

  - source: eBay

  - domain: ebay.com


  ## Content


  # Returns and refunds


  eBay groups buyer help for order problems into several related areas: items that
  have not arrived, returns, refunds, request status, and cases where eBay may need
  to step in.


  ## Returning an item


  A buyer who wants to return an item should start from the purchase record for that
  order. The return flow asks the buyer to identify the item and select a reason for
  the request. The return reason matters because it affects which rules apply and
  who may be responsible for return shipping.


  When a buyer has simply changed their mind, the listing is the place to check the
  seller''s return policy, including the available return window and any stated conditions.
  Seller return terms can differ between listings.


  When something is wrong with the item, the buyer should still use the order-linked
  return flow and choose the reason that accurately describes the problem. eBay provides
  separate help for items that are damaged, faulty, incorrect, or different from the
  listing.


  ## Request and review process


  After a request is opened, the seller is given an opportunity to respond and resolve
  the issue. The buyer can check the request status from the order record and follow
  any instructions about shipping the item back.


  If the seller does not respond or the issue is not resolved through the request,
  the buyer may be able to ask eBay to review the case. The relevant option and timing
  appear in the order or request details.


  ## Return shipping and refunds


  Responsibility for return shipping depends on both the seller''s stated policy and
  the reason for the return. Buyers should use the return method and deadline shown
  in the request. Refund timing may depend on the item being returned, delivery tracking,
  and the seller''s response.


  The return should be handled through the matching eBay order so the request, seller
  response, shipping information, and any later eBay review remain connected to the
  transaction.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Returns and refunds | eBay
url: https://www.ebay.com/help/returns-refunds

The applicable route is **eBay’s “item not as described” / wrong item process**, not a PayPal dispute.

### What I found for order EB-9009

- **Order:** Delivered July 8, 2026
- **Listing:** ProStore X2, **2 TB**, new
- **Received:** ProStore X1, **1 TB**, refurbished
- **Seller’s listing terms:** No ordinary returns
- **Payment:** Direct card through eBay’s marketplace processor
- **PayPal transaction:** None recorded
- **Existing requests/cases:** No return, seller contact, refund, eBay case, or payment dispute has been opened

The discrepancy is material, so the seller’s “no returns” setting for change-of-mind returns does **not necessarily remove eBay Money Back Guarantee coverage** for an item that is wrong or materially different from the listing. eBay says these requests should be started from the matching order and normally opened within 30 calendar days of delivery—here, that would generally be around **August 7, 2026**, subject to the transaction’s displayed deadline and any category-specific rules.

### Right next step

Open a return/request from **eBay order EB-9009** and select the most accurate reason such as:

> **Item not as described — received a different model, lower capacity, and refurbished condition instead of the advertised new 2 TB ProStore X2.**

Attach or preserve:

- Photos of the received drive and model/serial markings
- Evidence showing the 1 TB capacity
- Photos or screenshots showing its refurbished condition
- A screenshot of the original listing showing “ProStore X2,” “2 TB,” and “new”
- Packaging and shipping-label photos, if relevant

Wait for the seller’s response and follow any eBay-provided return-label and shipping instructions. If the seller does not resolve it, use the option in the eBay request to ask eBay to step in.

A PayPal dispute is not the right route here: the order record shows a **direct card payment with no PayPal transaction**. Sources: [eBay Money Back Guarantee policy](https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy?id=4210) and [eBay Returns and refunds](https://www.ebay.com/help/returns-refunds).

The applicable route is **eBay’s “item not as described” / wrong item process**, not a PayPal dispute.

### What I found for order EB-9009

- **Order:** Delivered July 8, 2026
- **Listing:** ProStore X2, **2 TB**, new
- **Received:** ProStore X1, **1 TB**, refurbished

- **Payment:** Direct card through eBay’s marketplace processor
- **PayPal transaction:** None recorded

The discrepancy is material, so the seller’s “no returns” setting for change-of-mind returns does **not necessarily remove eBay Money Back Guarantee coverage** for an item that is wrong or materially different from the listing. eBay says these requests should be started from the matching order and normally opened within 30 calendar days of delivery—here, that would generally be around **August 7, 2026**, subject to the transaction’s displayed deadline and any category-specific rules.

### Right next step

> **Item not as described — received a different model, lower capacity, and refurbished condition instead of the advertised new 2 TB ProStore X2.**

Wait for the seller’s response and follow any eBay-provided return-label and shipping instructions. If the seller does not resolve it, use the option in the eBay request to ask eBay to step in.

A PayPal dispute is not the right route here: the order record shows a **direct card payment with no PayPal transaction**.
